steps.label: Applicant Personal and Household Information Entry
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter your personal details and information about your household. Please provide accurate information for each required field. Instructions and guidance are provided for each section to help you complete your SNAP application. All fields marked with an asterisk (*) are mandatory. If you have questions about any field, hover over the info icon for more details.

fields.label: Social Security Number
fields.error_message: SSN must be in valid format.

fields.label: Income Sources
fields.error_message: Select at least one income source if income is provided.

data_validation: Birth date cannot be in the future
Household Size must be at least 1
If married, spouse information required in household members
Cannot have duplicate household member names
Phone number must be valid format
If disability claimed in income sources, at least one household member must have eligible status
Total number of household members must match Household Size

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to validate and submit your application

actions.name: Display Application Entry Screen
actions.implementation: When the workflow is initiated or the applicant returns from an error or notification screen, the system displays the application entry screen. All previously entered data is restored if available. The system logs the screen display event for audit purposes. If the applicant is returning from an error, highlight the fields that require correction.
actions.triggerCondition: Workflow step initiated or navigation from error/notification screen

actions.name: Handle Next Button Click
actions.implementation: When the applicant clicks the 'Next' button, the system initiates validation of all required fields (Name, Address, Household Size, Income, Citizenship Status, and other required fields as per data validation rules). If validation passes, proceed to application submission. If validation fails, trigger the error notification screen and display a summary of missing or invalid fields. Log the button click and validation attempt.

steps.label: Validate Required Fields
steps.description: System must verify that the following fields are not empty: Name, Address, Household Size, Income, and Citizenship Status. If any of these fields are empty, the application cannot be submitted and an error must be triggered.

rules.label: Required Fields Non-Empty Check
rules.logic: For each required field (Name, Address, Household Size, Income, Citizenship Status), check if the field value is not null, not undefined, and not an empty string. If any field fails this check, set status to 'error' and provide a message indicating which fields are incomplete.

actions.name: Handle Validation Failure
actions.implementation: If any required field is missing or invalid, set status to 'error', generate a message listing incomplete fields, and trigger the error notification screen (STEP_4). Log the validation failure event for compliance and tracking. Provide guidance for each missing or invalid field.
actions.triggerCondition: Validation of required fields fails after 'Next' button click

actions.name: Proceed to Application Submission
actions.implementation: If all required fields are valid, set status to 'success' and trigger the SNAP application submission rule. Log the successful validation event.
actions.triggerCondition: Validation of required fields passes after 'Next' button click

steps.label: Submit SNAP Application
steps.description: Upon successful validation of required fields, the system must save the application data, generate a unique application record, and log the submission event for compliance purposes. The transaction must remain open until all operations are completed successfully.

rules.label: Application Submission Transaction
rules.logic: If status is not 'error', initiate a transaction to save the application data, generate a unique application record, and log the submission event. Ensure the transaction remains open until all operations are complete. If a transaction error occurs (e.g., 'The result is out of scope. The associated transaction has been closed.'), set status to 'error' and provide the error message.

actions.name: Submit SNAP Application
actions.implementation: Initiate a transaction to save the application data, generate a unique application record, and log the submission event for compliance. If the transaction completes successfully, trigger the assignment step. If a transaction error occurs (e.g., database failure), set status to 'error', log the error with details, trigger an error handling action to notify system administrators, and display a user-friendly error message to the applicant via a dedicated error notification screen. The system determines the next step based on the transaction result.
actions.triggerCondition: Application submission initiated after successful validation

steps.label: Incomplete Fields Error Notification
steps.description: This screen displays a summary of required fields that are incomplete or missing. Review the error messages below and return to the previous screen to complete all required information. Guidance is provided for each missing field to help you correct your application. You cannot proceed until all required fields are completed.

fields.label: Missing or Incomplete Fields
fields.error_message: Please complete all required fields before submitting.

data_validation: Name, Address, Household Size, Income, and Citizenship Status must not be empty
All required fields must be completed before submission

buttons.label: Return to Application
buttons.description: Go back to the application entry screen to complete missing fields

actions.name: Display Incomplete Fields Error Notification
actions.implementation: When validation fails, the system displays the error notification screen summarizing missing or invalid fields. Guidance is provided for each missing field. The system logs the error notification event for compliance.
actions.triggerCondition: System receives validation failure status from STEP_2

actions.name: Handle Return to Application Button Click
actions.implementation: When the applicant clicks the 'Return to Application' button, the system navigates back to the application entry screen (STEP_1), restoring previously entered data for correction. Highlight the fields that require attention. Log the navigation event.

steps.label: Assign to Caseworker
steps.description: System routes the completed application record to the appropriate caseworker queue for further processing. The assignment ensures that each submitted and validated SNAP application is assigned to a caseworker based on predefined routing logic, such as workload balancing, applicant location, or caseworker specialization. The system must log the assignment event and update the application record with the assigned caseworker ID for tracking and compliance.

assignments.label: Assign to Caseworker
assignments.logic: Upon successful submission and validation of the SNAP application, the system evaluates routing criteria (e.g., applicant's address, caseworker workload, specialization) and assigns the application record to the appropriate caseworker queue. The system updates the application record with the assigned caseworker's identifier and logs the assignment event for audit and compliance purposes. If an error occurs during assignment (e.g., database transaction failure as indicated by the 'status' and 'message' fields), the system must handle the error gracefully and notify system administrators for resolution.

actions.name: Assign Application to Caseworker
actions.implementation: Upon successful application submission, the system evaluates routing criteria (e.g., applicant's address, caseworker workload, specialization) and assigns the application to the appropriate caseworker queue. Update the application record with the assigned caseworker's identifier, log the assignment event for audit and compliance. If an assignment error occurs (e.g., database transaction failure), set status to 'error', log the error, trigger an error handling action to notify system administrators, and display a user-friendly error message to the applicant via a dedicated error notification screen. The system determines the next step based on the assignment result.
actions.triggerCondition: Application submission transaction completes successfully in STEP_3

actions.name: Mark Workflow Completion and Notify Applicant
actions.implementation: After successful assignment to a caseworker, update the application status to 'Assigned', send a notification to the applicant (via email and/or dashboard notification) confirming submission and assignment, and log the workflow completion event for compliance and tracking. If notification fails, log the error and trigger an error handling action for system administrators. After notification, direct the applicant to the dashboard or confirmation screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Application record assigned to caseworker and event logged

steps.label: Caseworker Application Processing
steps.description: As a caseworker, you will see the details of the assigned SNAP application. Review the applicant's personal, household, and financial information. You may update the application status, add notes, or request additional information from the applicant. All actions are logged for compliance. Instructions and context for processing are provided at the top of the screen.
data_validation: Application Status must be selected before saving
Caseworker Comments must not contain prohibited language
Cannot approve application if required documents are missing
If status is 'More Information Requested', a comment is required

buttons.label: Save and Route
buttons.description: Save updates and route application for further processing

buttons.label: Request More Information
buttons.description: Send request to applicant for additional details

actions.name: Display Caseworker Application Processing Screen
actions.implementation: When a caseworker is assigned an application, the system displays the caseworker processing screen with all relevant application details. Log the screen display event for compliance.
actions.triggerCondition: Caseworker assignment event detected from STEP_5

actions.name: Handle Save and Route Button Click
actions.implementation: When the caseworker clicks the 'Save and Route' button, validate that the Application Status is selected and all required fields are complete. Save any updates to the application, log the action, and route the application to the next processing step or queue as appropriate. If validation fails, set status to 'error', display an error message via a dedicated error notification screen, and log the failure for compliance. After successful save and route, display a confirmation or move to the next processing step.

actions.name: Handle Request More Information Button Click
actions.implementation: When the caseworker clicks the 'Request More Information' button, ensure a comment is provided. Save the request, update the application status to 'More Information Requested', send a notification to the applicant (via email and/or dashboard), and log the action for compliance. If comment is missing, set status to 'error', display an error message via a dedicated error notification screen, and log the failure. After successful request, display a confirmation to the caseworker.
